commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin tool resolution by safely reading tool descriptor fields, pinning accepted tool names, adding malformed-descriptor diagnostics, and covering name/parameter edge cases in optional-tool tests.

PR surface: Source +121, Tests +259. Total +380 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection, not by running tests: `wrapPluginToolFactoryResult()` calls `isAgentTool()`, which reads `execute` directly before the new malformed-tool validator can catch and isolate the bad candidate.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can finish the missing execute-getter guard and add focused regression coverage; the protected maintainer label still leaves final merge handling to maintainers.

target.title: fix(plugins): guard plugin tool descriptor reads
target.body:
## Summary

- guard plugin tool descriptor reads so malformed `name`, `execute`, or `parameters` access cannot abort sibling tool resolution
- pin each accepted plugin tool to one stable name before allow/deny policy, manifest filtering, metadata, descriptor cache, and cached execution
- reject unstable or unpinnable tool names, including non-configurable accessors, descriptor traps, and writable non-configurable `name` properties

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed plugin tool descriptor access no longer crashes `resolvePluginTools()` or bypasses tool deny policy before healthy sibling tools resolve.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw worktree on Node via the repo Vitest wrapper, rebased onto `origin/main` commit `1c0fb5768be`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next181-rebased-final nodenv exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts --reporter=verbose
nodenv exec node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
blacksmith testbox list --all
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label next181-check-changed --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: `src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts` passed (`74 passed`), oxlint passed, diff-check passed, and branch autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` with `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`.

Observed result after fix: tools with unreadable `parameters` are diagnosed and skipped while valid siblings remain available; unreadable or unstable tool names are rejected before policy can be evaluated against a fallback name; accepted tools expose the same stable name through registration and cached execution.

What was not tested: broad `pnpm check:changed` was blocked by maintainer runner auth. `blacksmith testbox list --all` failed with `not authenticated -- run 'blacksmith auth login' first`; AWS Crabbox failed before lease creation with coordinator `POST /v1/runs` and `POST /v1/leases` returning HTTP 401.

## Red proof

Before the fix, the new unreadable-parameters regression crashed with:

```text
broken_tool parameters exploded
```

Autoreview also caught and drove fixes for policy-sensitive name instability: thrown `name` reads, changing names, non-configurable accessor names, descriptor traps, and writable non-configurable names.
target.author: vincentkoc
